title: Chhatarpur Temple
tagline: Chhatarpur Temple — spiritual town of Delhi

overview.short: Shri Aadya Katyayani Shakti Peeth is a Hindu temple dedicated to the Hindu deity Katyayani.
overview.description: Shri Aadya Katyayani Shakti Peeth is a Hindu temple dedicated to the Hindu deity Katyayani. The entire complex of the temple is spread over a wide area of 28 hectares (70 acres). It is located in Chhatarpur, on the southwestern outskirts of the New Delhi. It is the 2nd largest temple in Delhi, after the Akshardham Temple.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chhatarpur metro station
topPlaces.description: Chhatarpur is an elevated station on the Yellow Line of the Delhi Metro. It is located in the Chhatarpur locality of the South West district of Delhi, India.

topPlaces.name: Qutab Minar metro station
topPlaces.description: Qutab Minar is an elevated station on the Yellow Line of the Delhi Metro. It was inaugurated on 21 June 2010 as part of the 14.47 km completely elevated corridor from Qutab Minar – HUDA City Centre.

topPlaces.name: Hauz-i-Shamsi
topPlaces.description: Hauz-i-Shamsi (literally "sunny water tank"), also known as Shamsi Talab, is a water reservoir built by Iltutmish of the Slave Dynasty in 1230 CE. According to legend, its location was revealed to him in a dream by the Islamic prophet Muhammad. A palace known as the Jahaz Mahal was constructed on the eastern edge of the same reservoir during the Lodi dynasty in the 16th century, serving as a retreat or inn for pilgrims.

topPlaces.name: Jahaz Mahal
topPlaces.description: Jahaz Mahal (lit. 'Ship Palace') is located next to Hauz-i-Shamsi in Mehrauli, Delhi. It was so named, since its reflection (illusion) in the surrounding reservoir looked like a ship floating on a lake.

topPlaces.name: Sultanpur metro station
topPlaces.description: The Sultanpur metro station is located on the Yellow Line of the Delhi Metro. During Peak hours, it serves as an alternate southern Terminus for the yellow line along with Qutab Minar.

topPlaces.name: Sultanpur, Delhi
topPlaces.description: Sultanpur is a village in South Delhi district of Delhi. According to the 2011 census, there are 3280 houses, two government schools and three national banks.

topPlaces.name: Satbari
topPlaces.description: Satbari (also spelled as Satbadi) is a village and census town located in the Chhatarpur area of South Delhi district in Delhi, India. Its postal code is 110074.

topPlaces.name: Mehrauli
topPlaces.description: Mehrauli is a neighbourhood in South Delhi, Delhi, India. It represents a constituency in the Delhi Legislative Assembly.

According to legend, its location was revealed to him in a dream by the Islamic prophet Muhammad. A palace known as the Jahaz Mahal was constructed on the eastern edge of the same reservoir during the Lodi dynasty in the 16th century, serving as a retreat or inn for pilgrims.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chhatarpur Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chhatarpur Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chhatarpur Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (11 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (17 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chhatarpur Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chhatarpur Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chhatarpur Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Chhatarpur Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chhatarpur Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chhatarpur metro station, Qutab Minar metro station, Hauz-i-Shamsi, Jahaz Mahal, Sultanpur metro station.
